generated_note: Hand-maintained registry. 'enabled' stations are the only ones historical_data.py/station_features.py may actually fetch from. Every 'enabled: false' entry is an unverified candidate - proposed from general knowledge of the Swiss station network, never confirmed against a live provider API, and must never be treated as real coverage. See docs/STATION_RESEARCH.md for the narrative version of this same list, including rejected candidates.

stations.name: Samedan
stations.licence: MeteoSwiss Open Data (opendata.swiss terms of use - attribution required, commercial use permitted)
stations.notes: 10km from the Silvaplana target lake, in the Upper Engadin (same broad target region as the lake). fu3010h0/fu3010h1 columns confirmed against the live MeteoSwiss STAC API. Historical fallback ground truth for backtest.py labeling (kitesailing.ch has no archive); real lake correlation is only ~0.52, an acknowledged accuracy ceiling - see CLAUDE.md.

stations.name: Lugano
stations.licence: MeteoSwiss Open Data (opendata.swiss terms of use)
stations.notes: Southern term of the Lugano-Zurich pressure-gradient signal already used by features.py (pressure_signal, pressure_nowcast_score). pp0qffh0 (QFF sea-level pressure) confirmed against the live API.

stations.name: Zürich / Fluntern
stations.licence: MeteoSwiss Open Data (opendata.swiss terms of use)
stations.notes: Northern term of the same pressure-gradient signal. pp0qffh0 confirmed against the live API.

stations.name: Piz Corvatsch
stations.licence: MeteoSwiss Open Data (opendata.swiss terms of use) - Piz Corvatsch (official abbreviation COV) is a confirmed official MeteoSwiss automatic station
stations.notes: CONFIRMED via two real, network-enabled GitHub Actions runs (2026-07-16/17): (1) `historical_data.py sync --station cov` returned 398,816 real hourly records, 1981-01-01 through the run date, matching Samedan's own record depth almost exactly (data_since per the official metadata is actually 1979-07-26 - archive coverage starts slightly later); (2) `historical_data.py metadata --station cov` returned the real official coordinates/elevation above (lat 46.418039, lon 9.821308, elev 3294m - very close to the ~3294-3297m approximate figure previously in docs/DATA_ARCHITECTURE.md, now superseded by this confirmed value). available_variables lists only the fields with DIRECT evidence of real non-null values (from historical_data.py validate's per-flag-type breakdown: implausible_global_radiation_wm2, implausible_relative_humidity_pct, implausible_sunshine_duration_min all fired on real data, and gust_less_than_speed firing confirms both wind_speed_ms and wind_gust_ms are populated). wind_direction_deg is standard SwissMetNet wind instrumentation (dkl010h0, the direction companion to the confirmed speed/gust columns) but has no independent flag-based confirmation of its own yet - a value out of [0,360] would flag, but a correctly-populated field never would, so absence of a flag doesn't confirm presence the way it does for the other fields above. temperature_c/dew_point_c/precipitation_mm/pressure_sea_level_hpa may also be published but aren't yet confirmed present either. reporting_delay_minutes=15 remains the conservative unmeasured default carried over from before confirmation - not yet independently measured. See docs/STATION_RESEARCH.md for the full investigation history (the earlier 403 ProxyError blocks were a sandbox limitation, not a real access restriction - MeteoSwiss's open data is unrestricted).

stations.name: Passo del Bernina
stations.licence: MeteoSwiss Open Data (opendata.swiss terms of use) - Passo del Bernina (official abbreviation BEH) is a confirmed official MeteoSwiss automatic station
stations.notes: IDENTITY CONFIRMED via a real `historical_data.py metadata --search bernina` run (2026-07-17, GitHub Actions): Passo del Bernina is a genuine, official MeteoSwiss automatic station, official abbreviation BEH (not a guess - discovered via meteoswiss.search_stations_by_name(), never an invented code), coordinates/elevation above are the real official values, data_since 1908-11-01 (an even longer record than Samedan's). What remains unconfirmed is an actual historical data fetch (`historical_data.py sync --station beh`) and which variables it publishes - deliberately deferred to a later sync, not attempted this session. Bernina Pass sits east of the Engadin on the route toward Poschiavo/Italy - candidate roles here mirror the existing (still-unconfirmed-abbreviation) 'poschiavo' entry's eastern/Bernina-flow-suppression rationale, but at the pass itself rather than the valley town beyond it. Do not enable until a real sync succeeds and a human inspects the result - same bar as every other station in this registry.

stations.name: Piz Nair (St. Moritz)
stations.notes: No station code could be proposed with any confidence. May only be SLF/IMIS or ski-operator instrumented, not MeteoSwiss.

stations.name: Maloja
stations.notes: One of Switzerland's oldest continuously operated climate stations (long precipitation/temperature record) - moderate confidence a MeteoSwiss station exists here, but the exact current API station code was not confirmed against a live fetch.

stations.name: Sils / Segl (Silser See)
stations.licence: unknown - user-provided export; original data source and licence terms not stated, not MeteoSwiss
stations.notes: Lake immediately upstream of Silvaplana. Not a MeteoSwiss station - no live/API access exists or is expected (see historical_data.NO_LIVE_SOURCE_STATIONS). Confirmed via a real user-provided historical CSV (weatherdata_silser_see_*.csv, semicolon-delimited, knots wind, station-level pressure), ingested 2026-07-17 via `historical_data.py import-csv --station sils --format semicolon_weather` into logs/raw_cache/generic_sils.json: 22 real hourly records, all from 2014-04-02. A one-day sample, not a multi-year archive - more files for this or other stations may be imported later the same way; import-csv merges rather than overwrites.

stations.name: Vicosoprano
stations.notes: IMPORTANT: these coordinates are already used in features.py as an Open-Meteo FORECAST-MODEL grid point, not a real ground station. No evidence was found that Vicosoprano hosts an actual MeteoSwiss station - do not conflate the two.

stations.name: Poschiavo
stations.notes: Proposed as an eastern/Bernina-flow-suppression context station - station code not confirmed against a live fetch.

stations.name: Chur
stations.notes: Well-known long-record station north of the Engadin - moderate confidence it exists in the current API under some code, not confirmed this session.
